reasoning:
The question resolves based on whether Reserve Balances of Depository Institutions (WLODLL) increase between the March 11, 2026 data release (the value representing Mar 15) and the March 18, 2026 release (the value representing Mar 22). Structurally, on the 15th of every month, the US Treasury settles significant mid-month bond auctions (3-year, 10-year, 30-year). With large ongoing federal deficits, the Treasury typically drains net liquidity on these settlement dates to fund the Treasury General Account (TGA), mechanically reducing Reserve Balances. 

An analysis of the weekly FRED data provided strongly corroborates this mid-month settlement drain:
- Week of Jan 14 to Jan 21 (capturing Jan 15 settlement): Reserves plummeted from 3,061k to 2,956k.
- Week of Dec 10 to Dec 17 (capturing Dec 15): Reserves fell from 2,973k to 2,933k.
- Week of Feb 11 to Feb 18 (capturing Feb 17 clearing): Reserves stalled completely, rising a negligible +1k, abruptly pausing an otherwise aggressive +45k to +60k weekly uptrend.

For March 2026, the mid-month 15th falls on a Sunday. Settlement clears Monday, March 16, accompanied by any corresponding March 15 pass-through and estimated tax remittances, directly swelling the TGA. The subsequent observation date falls on Wednesday, March 18. This practically guarantees massive headwind pressures on reserves right at the target week. Despite recent positive momentum pushing reserves to 3.07T by March 11 (likely a temporary pre-settlement peak inflated by tax refunds), the mid-month structural drain makes a sequential increase highly improbable. Therefore, the probability leans heavily toward a week-over-week decrease.
